caption: Discipline scales how long a rout takes -- it no longer grants near-immunity (#529). Team 0's lone, jittery infantry (morale 15) trades blows head-on with an equal enemy infantry; sustained frontal casualties erode its morale to 0 and it breaks and flees (drawn faded). Before this fix, in-fight morale recovery (well-trained melee units rotate fresh files to the front) could outpace casualty erosion indefinitely, so a disciplined unit basically never routed from attrition alone -- it just ground on until wiped out. Now recovery fades as the regiment thins (fewer fresh files left to rotate) and cuts off once it's badly bloodied, so erosion always wins eventually: every unit has a reachable rout threshold, higher discipline just costs the attacker more time/losses to reach it. The unit breaks contact once routing (untargetable), then rallies back under control at low morale once its nerve steadies -- still in the fight, not an instant loss.
